title: Review these policy files for a recovery-first change to Kernel fallback behavior. Goal: avoid immediate BLOCKED when baseline lanes are missing; require concrete fallback/recovery attempts first, and only BLOCK after those attempts fail with evidence. Focus on consistency gaps and exact wording ris...
summary_text: **Findings** 1. High: internal contradiction in `AGENTS.md` can still permit immediate fail-fast before recovery attempts. [AGENTS.md:92](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/AGENTS.md:92) says lane resolution “fails fast,” while [AGENTS.md:122](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/AGENTS.md:122)-[129](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/AGENTS.md:129) requires recovery-first. 2. High: trio gate wording can bypass recovery-first unless precedence is explicit. [AGENTS.md:107](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/AGENTS.md:107) enforces trio success before execution approval, but does not explicitly say “after recovery matrix attempts.” 3. Medium: blocker handling order is ambiguous in shared playbook. [rules/shared-orchestration-playbook.md:79](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/rules/shared-orchestration-playbook.md:79) says “return `BLOCKED` or continue correction,” then [80](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/rules/shared-orchestration-playbook.md:80)-[82](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/rules/shared-orchestration-playbook.md:82) adds recovery-first. The first line can be read as immediate `BLOCKED`. 4. Medium: recovery trigger scope is inconsistent (specific lanes vs baseline-any-lane). Prompts/CODEX focus on “missing Claude or GLM” ([kernel.md:52](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/.codex/prompts/kernel.md:52), [vote.md:44](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/.codex/prompts/vote.md:44), [CODEX.md:69](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/CODEX.md:69)); `AGENTS.md` uses broader “missing baseline provider or lane-floor shortfall” ([AGENTS.md:122](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/AGENTS.md:122)). 5. Medium: fallback quorum/lane-floor criteria are referenced but not concretely defined in one place. “valid fallback quorum” is required ([AGENTS.md:128](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/AGENTS.md:128), [131](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/AGENTS.md:131)), but mode-level floors differ across docs ([AGENTS.md:91](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/AGENTS.md:91), [kernel.md:42](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/.codex/prompts/kernel.md:42), [vote.md:50](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/.codex/prompts/vote.md:50)). 6. Low: terminology drift raises implementation risk (`recovery matrix` vs `recovery pass`, `approved fallback provider` vs `approved fallback lane`). Examples: [AGENTS.md:123](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/AGENTS.md:123), [kernel.md:33](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/.codex/prompts/kernel.md:33), [rules/shared-orchestration-playbook.md:80](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/rules/shared-orchestration-playbook.md:80). **Recommended Wording Themes** 1. Precedence lock: “No `BLOCKED` for lane/provider absence until recovery matrix is attempted and evidenced in current run.” 2. Universal trigger scope: apply recovery-first to any baseline lane loss and lane-floor shortfall, not only Claude/GLM. 3. Single canonical recovery sequence across all files: re-probe -> approved fallback promote -> lane-floor backfill -> evidence capture. 4. Explicit fallback quorum definition by mode (kernel bootstrap, kernel handoff, vote strict, continuity read-only). 5. Uniform evidence schema for failed recovery attempts (attempted path, timestamp/run id, exact error, decision outcome).
